title: AI Hackers Are Targeting Ordinary People Now, Not Just Big Tech | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
summary: Damien Lewke, CEO of Nebulock, argues that AI has dramatically lowered the barrier to cyberattacks, reducing the resource and expertise required from elite teams with years of experience to just two people with a GPU. He presents the cyber kill chain stage by stage, identifies three behavioral signals of insider breaches, and advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture as the only realistic security model today.
writeup: Damien Lewke, CEO of Nebulock, argues that AI has collapsed the cost of cyberattacks, taking what once required elite teams with years of experience down to two people and a GPU, which extends the threat from large enterprises to ordinary targets. The video walks through the cyber kill chain stage by stage and identifies three behavioral signals that indicate insider breaches. The central recommendation is an "assume breach" posture, treating compromise as already present rather than something to be kept out, paired with active threat hunting.
